---
title: "Eastern Bering Sea pollock stock assessment"
subtitle: "Annual assessment workspace"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Current assessment

The [2026 assessment workspace](assessment-years/2026/index.qmd) is now active.
The working schedule is an early September 2026 draft followed by the final
assessment in November 2026. There was no operational assessment in 2025.

The 2026 bridge therefore has two documented anchors:

1. the accepted 2024 assessment; and
2. methodological work conducted during 2025 in response to the CIE review and
   related September drafts.

New data are incorporated only after delivery and validation. The 2026 data
inventory distinguishes candidate files already located on disk from pending
survey and annual products.

## 2024 framework example

This compact example shows how a real Alaska groundfish assessment can be
represented with explicit annual provenance, model-run roles, review decisions,
and final deliverables. It is based on the committed 2024 EBS pollock assessment
at [`41dc244`](https://github.com/afsc-assessments/ebs_pollock_safe/commit/41dc244ade54692282b1ac530b76757d4687fd37).

## Annual model sequence

| Run | Role | Change from parent | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `m23.ly` | Bridge | Prior Model 23 with prior-year data | Comparison |
| `m23` | Updated base | Adds new AVO, BTS, ATS, catch, and fishery-age data | Author-recommended |
| `m1` | Data withholding | Excludes new BTS age and biomass inputs | Comparison |
| `m2` | Data withholding | Additionally excludes new ATS inputs | Comparison |
| `m3` | Data withholding | Additionally excludes new AVO input | Comparison |

The full assessment states that the sequential survey-data exclusions produced
relatively minor spawning-biomass changes and associates the principal change
from the 2023 result with the updated 2023 fishery catch-at-age information.

## Supporting diagnostics

The Model 23 directory contains separate retrospective, likelihood-profile, and
MCMC workflows. The assessment reports a maximum gradient below `1e-3`, R-hat
below 1.01, and effective sample sizes above 400 for key parameters. A production
release should link each reported diagnostic to the exact output and record a
reviewer, date, and pass/fail decision.

## Projection connection

Model 23 has a separate `proj/` directory containing projection inputs and
machine-readable summaries. The deliverables manifest identifies the expected
connection between the terminal assessment state, Tier 3 projections, and the
OFL/ABC values reported in the SAFE.

## Deliberately visible gaps

`TO_CONFIRM` and `incomplete` entries are retained where the public assessment
snapshot does not establish an authoritative owner, upstream delivery identifier,
redistribution decision, or final review disposition. Making these gaps visible
is preferable to inferring provenance during succession.

## Why this extends the generic template

The generic `R/`, `data-raw/`, `data/`, `scripts/`, and `report/` structure is
useful, but an operational Alaska example also needs:

- annual model and data manifests;
- explicit bridge and alternative-model relationships;
- assessment-to-projection traceability;
- Plan Team and SSC decision records;
- separate handling of restricted data and shareable metadata;
- release validation for the values entering the SAFE.
